sections.title: Consumer Apps
sections.subtitle: A curated portfolio of consumer applications I've had the opportunity to work on across the B2C healthcare and technology industries. Click on any tile to learn more about each project.

cards.title: Scheduling: Set Your Working Hours
cards.problem: Our marketplace is supply-constrained because we ask therapists to elect their hours, and we're only showing 25% of the time slots our competitors are showing.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Doubled the amount of therapist availability per week
Secondary: $2M in ARR
cards.solution: Therapists set working hours, and we generate bookable time slots for clients. Anything outside of busy times from Google Calendar within working hours is considered a time slot.

cards.title: Scheduling: Enter Your Availability in 3 Clicks
cards.problem: High-friction availability creation (12 clicks per timeslot in the EHR) artifically constraints supply and causes therapist frustration.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Added 2 hours of therapist availability per week
Secondary: $1M in ARR
cards.solution: Therapists can update their availability in just 3 clicks, directly from the new calendar app in the Therapist Portal. The calendar shows both Google and EHR appointments in one place, decreasing the time to add new availabilty by 75%.

cards.title: Daily Dashboard for Therapists
cards.problem: Workflows spread across 4+ disparate systems; current daily email has stale data and <10% click rate despite >85% open rate.
cards.solution: Therapists can start their day with real-time insights served over a dashboard with caseload insights, one-click access to telehealth appointments, and actionable metrics.

cards.title: QA: Bulk Clinical Note Review
cards.problem: It takes 6 months for therapists to receive feedback from our QA team, because each therapist's case requires 2 hours of manual review.
cards.solution: Our QA team can now review clinical notes, detect duplicate notes, and automatically email therapists with feedback from the same place.

cards.title: Manage Your Caseload
cards.problem: Therapists navigate 10+ disconnected systems to find basic client info; no accurate centralized caseload view in Therapist Portal.
cards.solution: Therapists can see a list of their active and past clients, including recommended next steps and when their last appointment with their client was.

cards.title: Multi-State Practice
cards.problem: Therapists licensed in multiple states can only practice in one, limiting supply in new markets and national payer attractiveness.
cards.metrics: Increase supply within our existing network by 10%
cards.solution: Our Therapist Portal and EHR now supports updating schedules and billing claims for multiple states. This expands the number of covered lives per therapist.

cards.title: In-Person Appointments
cards.problem: 40%+ of therapy clients prefer in-person care, but we don't offer any in-person appointments.
cards.metrics: Increase supply within our existing network by 15%
cards.solution: Our EHR and provider-facing tooling now displays in-person appointment options. We updated our billing system to differentiate between in-person and telehealth appointment claims.

sections.title: Business Integrations
sections.subtitle: A curated portfolio of business integrations I've had the opportunity to work on across the B2C healthcare and technology industries. Click on any tile to learn more about each project.

cards.title: Google Calendar Integration
cards.problem: Therapists double-book with competitors because our appointments aren't visible on their Google Calendar.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Doubled the amount of therapist availability per week
Secondary: $2M in ARR
cards.solution: Therapists now have access to an outbound Google Calendar sync to write appointments back to the therapist's Google Calendar. This allows therapists to see their entire work schedule from Google Calendar, decreasing the chance that they will be double-booked with competitors. We support up to 9 calendars and 3 Google accounts.

cards.title: Zendesk Support Chatbot
cards.problem: After a major workflow change, we saw a 5× increase in therapist-submitted support tickets. With only Slack to triage requests, our operational teams were severely bottlenecked.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Therapist NPS
Secondary: Decrease support tickets by 20%
cards.solution: Therapists can now use a Zendesk live chat widget in the Therapist Portal which decreased support ticket volume and improved therapist satisfaction.

cards.title: Streamlined Zendesk Support Queues
cards.problem: Therapists request support via Gmail and Slack, creating untrackable, unsecured requests with long turnaround times.
cards.solution: Therapists can message staff via the Therapist Portal, which creates a Zendesk message. This scales to our support volume of 2000+ tickets/month and allows staff to triage replies.

cards.title: Therapist Database: A Single Source of Truth
cards.problem: Therapist data lacks a single source of truth, fragmented across Airtable, Zoho, and Zapier with no defined schema — causing recurring sync failures, operational brittleness, and reliance on manual file uploads.
cards.metrics: Primary: Number of systems required to process a new therapist record
cards.solution: We built a new relational SQL database as the internal source of truth for therapist data, standardizing the data coming from the upstream Airtable integration. This new database is a single point of truth for therapist data across all therapist directories and internal booking pages, replacing the fragmented Airtable, Zoho, and Zapier integrations.

cards.title: Onboard Partner Therapist Network
cards.problem: We needed to onboard 10K+ therapists from a new partnership network, but our current onboarding process didn't scale to this volume.
cards.solution: We reused our exisitng Tellescope CRM instance to build no-code workflows for therapists to complete enrollment, automate operational workflows (background checks, credentialing, etc.), and receive branded communications.

cards.title: Faster New Hire Onboarding
cards.problem: Therapist onboarding requires therapists to navigate 6+ systems, lacks the visibility for staff to trace progress end-to-end, and takes 47 days on average. The process is cumbersome enough to cause significant attrition.
cards.solution: We implemented a bidirectional sync between our Therapist Database and Tellescope CRM, where Tellescope is used as an environment to store therapist lead data. This enables us to retire Airtable upstream and integrate Tellescope as the only CRM for provider information.

cards.title: Faster Invoice Processing
cards.problem: Since we run a bimonthly payroll, therapists get paid on different days of the week. Our contracted therapists rely on consistent pay dates.
cards.solution: All therapists are now paid biweekly on the same dates each month. This updated schedule improves therapist satisfaction and reduced the time to pay by 25%.

cards.title: Streamline Payment Infrastructure For Faster Payouts
cards.problem: Each appointment required 6+ hours to sync between our systems before it could be processed for payment, requiring 15+ hrs of manual intervention per pay period.
cards.metrics:
Primary: Time to sync (6 hrs → 20 minutes)
Secondary: Reduce ZD tickets by 50%
cards.solution: We reduced the time to sync updates to appointments by 95% by creating a new microservice that syncs appointment data to the payment processing system.

cards.title: CRM for White-Glove Client Leads
cards.problem: To match a single client to a therapist, our white-glove care navigation team used 5+ spreadsheets to track client info and preferences. This process was tedious, taking 2 hours to match a client to a therapist. Our teams also couldn't track a client's progress through care after the initial match.
cards.solution: Tellescope CRM now replaces the matching spreadsheets with an agentic ticketing workflow, reducing the time to match a client to a therapist by 75%.
